opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: A mole on my back has been growing really fast, and tonight it broke open and won't stop bleeding no matter how much I press on it.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The spot ulcerated after I dried off with a towel and has been oozing for about two hours; I live alone and can't reach it properly to hold pressure.

symptom_narrative.onset: the mole started changing about 3 months ago; the bleeding started tonight after minor friction from a towel
symptom_narrative.duration: 3 months of change; roughly 2 hours of bleeding tonight
symptom_narrative.character: the spot has been enlarging quickly and looks patchy and uneven in colour; tonight the middle broke open and is oozing dark red blood
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the bleeding is steady and keeps restarting; I feel anxious and can't get comfortable
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on my upper back, on the left side, an area I can't really reach
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the change has been gradual over months; the bleeding has been continuous since it opened tonight
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: pressing on it slows it a bit but I can't reach to hold it firmly
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any rubbing or contact restarts the bleeding
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: it has started itching over the last few weeks; it looks different from every other mole I have
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: it has never bled before tonight
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm sitting leaning forward so nothing touches my back, and I'm too anxious to sleep

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: otherwise generally well, no ongoing medical problems that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I spent a lot of time out in the sun without cover when I was young; I live on my own

objective_data_offered.value: a dark, uneven-coloured spot on my upper back, a bit over a centimetre across, with a raw open centre that is oozing dark red blood; the edges look ragged
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-held phone photo over chat; lighting and focus limit what can be judged and it is no substitute for an in-person skin examination

objective_data_offered.value: it's been oozing dark red blood for about two hours and has soaked through a couple of towels; it keeps coming back whenever I let go
when you asked me to check, I can feel a firm lump in my left armpit
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-palpation only; not a clinical examination

objective_data_offered.value: I don't feel dizzy, faint or short of breath, and I'm not feeling my heart racing
